codeReview.summary: The rerun handoff still fails review. The repository contains a real fix for the Stop timer regression, but this feature attributes the result to refactor commit 45e0674 instead of the actual stabilization commit 94f7496, so the scoped fix evidence still does not satisfy the feature requirement.

issues.description: The handoff reports commit 45e0674 as the implementation commit even though that diff only refactors WorkspaceTimerPage into useTimerPageOrchestration and adds unrelated docs/research assets. It does not touch apps/website/e2e/timer-page.spec.ts or apps/website/e2e/fixtures/e2e-auth.ts, where the Stop timer stabilization actually landed.

The running-timer assertion path was restored by switching workspace-change poll callbacks to pollSessionBootstrap and keeping Stop timer assertions intact, but those changes live in commit 94f7496 rather than the reviewed feature commit. The rerun therefore still lacks a scoped implementation unit that directly contains the claimed fix.

sharedStateObservations.observation: frontend-test-worker guidance already says rerun-only success is not enough for a flaky-fix and that the handoff commit must directly contain the fix or explicitly say the behavior was already fixed earlier. The worker transcript identified 94f7496 as the real pollSessionBootstrap stabilization but still handed off unrelated refactor commit 45e0674 as the feature commit.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/frontend-test-worker/SKILL.md steps 10-11 require direct commit traceability for fixes; worker transcript for session cf4b2c45-e870-4d52-b347-41f05142563f states "the issue has been addressed by the previous worker's commit 94f7496" before ending the feature run with commitId 45e067442aa1cbb68ce0d36c48e29384b9fcdcdb.

summary: Fail: the Stop timer regression is green in the repo, but this rerun still does not provide scoped fix evidence because it points to unrelated refactor commit 45e0674 instead of the actual pollSessionBootstrap stabilization in 94f7496.
